steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: The blank is part of an independent clause with a singular subject (noun phrase “The extensor digitorum brevis” with a parenthetical relative clause) and a direct object (noun phrase “responsibility for extending the middle toes”).

steps.title: Select the finite verb form
steps.content: An independent clause requires a finite verb as its main predicate. “Bears” is the only finite verb form — third-person singular present tense for the singular subject. The other options are non-finite forms (two participles and one infinitive) that cannot function as a main verb. This matches choice C — C is correct.
